items.front: When is radar identification transfer NOT required? (5-4-1)
items.back: Where it is not operationally feasible. The constraint must be covered in an LOA stating control will not be based on a radar handoff, or coordinated by the controllers for a specified period.

items.front: Handoff vs. Point Out vs. Traffic (5-4-2)
items.back: HANDOFF: transfers radar ID AND communications. POINT OUT: transfers radar ID, communications NOT transferred. TRAFFIC: transfers radar ID to coordinate separation action. RADAR CONTACT: handoff acknowledgment. POINT OUT APPROVED: point out acknowledgment. TRAFFIC OBSERVED: traffic acknowledgment.

items.front: Three circumstances where Traffic is normally issued (5-4-2e)
items.back: 1. In response to a handoff or point out. 2. In anticipation of a handoff or point out. 3. In conjunction with a request for control of an aircraft.

items.front: Four approved methods for radar ID transfer (5-4-3a)
items.back: 1. Physically point to target on receiving controller's display. 2. Landline voice communications. 3. Automation capabilities. 4. TERMINAL: Modify/Quick Look functions between TRACON and tower (only with facility directive and local controller determining STARS data adequacy on TDW).

items.front: Information relay order for handoff/point out/traffic (5-4-3b)
items.back: 1. Target position relative to a known fix/map symbol/radar target. 2. Aircraft ID (call sign, discrete beacon code for interfacility point outs with agreement, or CID for en route intrafacility point outs). 3. Assigned altitude, restrictions, climb/descent if applicable. 4. Other pertinent information not in data block or flight data.

items.front: Response phraseology for handoff/point out/traffic (5-4-3c)
items.back: (Aircraft ID) (restrictions) RADAR CONTACT, OR (ID) (restrictions) POINT OUT APPROVED, OR TRAFFIC OBSERVED, OR UNABLE (appropriate information as required).

items.front: Physical pointing exception (5-4-3b note)
items.back: When physically pointing to the target, you do not have to state the aircraft position.

items.front: Two responsibilities when using 'Traffic' for separation (5-4-4)
items.back: 1. Controller issuing traffic MUST issue appropriate restrictions. 2. Controller accepting restrictions MUST ensure approved separation is maintained between involved aircraft.

items.front: Transferring controller handoff requirements (5-4-5)
items.back: Complete handoff BEFORE aircraft enters receiving delegated airspace. Verbally obtain approval before changing flight path/altitude/speed/data block during/after handoff. Advise of pertinent info not in data block. Initiate verbal coordination for primary/nondiscrete targets. Resolve conflicts and comply with restrictions before transferring communications.

items.front: Data block indicators requiring verbal coordination before control transfer (5-4-5e)
items.back: CST, FAIL, NONE, IF, NT, TRK.

items.front: Pre-communications-transfer checklist for transferring controller (5-4-5h)
items.back: 1. Resolve adjacent-airspace violations and conflicts in your area. 2. Coordinate with controllers whose airspace aircraft transits before destination. 3. Forward any separation restrictions to receiving controller. 4. Comply with restrictions from receiving controller.

items.front: When is the target considered identified on receiving controller's display (5-4-5g)
items.back: When the receiving controller acknowledges receipt verbally or accepts the automated handoff.

items.front: Receiving controller data block indicator actions (5-4-6f)
items.back: AMB/AM: advise other facility of computer-to-computer position disparity. NAT/NT: advise other facility IF disparity exists between declared position and actual target position. DATA/CST/NONE/OLD: initiate verbal coordination. ERAM MISM: notify OS/CIC.

items.front: Nondiscrete beacon target post-handoff confirmation (5-4-6d)
items.back: Observe a code change, an ident reply, or a standby squawk — unless one of these was already used during the handoff.

items.front: Three ways beacon target identity is confirmed (5-4-6e)
items.back: 1. Data block shows computer-assigned discrete beacon code is being received. 2. Observe deletion of a discrete code from data block. 3. Observe numeric display of a discrete code aircraft was instructed to squawk or reports squawking.

items.front: Point out requirements for transferring controller (5-4-7a)
items.back: Obtain approval before aircraft enters receiving delegated airspace. EN ROUTE: revert to verbal if receiving controller takes no action. Obtain approval before changing flight path/altitude/speed/data block after point out approved. Comply with receiving controller's restrictions. Responsible for subsequent handoffs and comm transfer unless otherwise agreed.

items.front: Point out requirements for receiving controller (5-4-7b)
items.back: Ensure target position matches transferring controller's info or computer association exists before approving. Responsible for separation between point out aircraft and other aircraft under their responsibility. Issue restrictions necessary to provide separation within their area of jurisdiction.

items.front: Three conditions for AIT without verbal coordination (5-4-8)
items.back: 1. During radar handoff. 2. Via information displayed in full data blocks. 3. Following procedures specified in facility AIT directive and/or LOA.

items.front: When is prearranged coordination approved? (5-4-9)
items.back: Only when procedures are established and published in a facility directive in accordance with JO 7210.3, paragraph 3-6-6. Under no circumstances may one controller permit an aircraft to enter another's airspace without proper coordination.

items.front: Five proper means of coordination (5-4-9 note)
items.back: 1. Radar handoff. 2. Automated information transfer. 3. Verbal. 4. Point out. 5. Prearranged coordination procedures in a facility directive.

items.front: En route fourth line heading format (5-4-10d)
items.back: H followed by three-digit number (e.g., H080, H270). PH may denote present heading. H may be omitted only if necessary due to character field limitations.

items.front: Weather deviation format in fourth line (5-4-10f)
items.back: D=deviation. Direction: L=left, R=right, N=north, E=east, S=south, W=west. /F=direct next NAVAID/waypoint in flight plan. /(fix)=rejoin at specific fix. Two digits=turn degrees with direction. Three digits=heading(s). D(heading)-(heading)=deviate between headings.

items.front: Airspeed format in fourth line (5-4-10g-h)
items.back: S preceding three-digit number for airspeeds (S210). Ending '+' means at or greater (250+). Ending '-' means at or less (280-). Mach: M, M., or '.' preceding two-digit value (M80, M.80, .80, .80-).

items.front: Requesting altitude or route change in fourth line (5-4-10j-k)
items.back: Altitude: RQ preceding three-digit number (RQ170). Route: RQ/ preceding specific fix identifier (RQ/LAX, RQ/NEUTO).

items.front: Fourth line modification rule after handoff acceptance (5-4-10l)
items.back: Information must not be modified outside the controller's area of jurisdiction unless verbally coordinated or specified in an LOA or Facility Directive. Receiving controller must advise if information is not understood or needs revision.
